text: Git branching is a core part of version control that helps developers work on code without disrupting the main project. In a typical version control workflow, each developer creates a "feature branch" to work on a new function or fix. This keeps the main code stable while changes are tested. Once the feature is ready, the developer submits a "pull request" to propose merging their branch back into the main branch. Teammates can review the code, suggest improvements, and discuss changes before the merge. After approval, the branch is merged, combining the work. This process makes collaboration safe, organized, and efficient.
